diagrams.title: Phong Reflection Model Components
diagrams.description: Shows the three components of Phong model: Ambient (uniform), Diffuse (angle-dependent), and Specular (view-dependent highlight)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how three lighting components combine to create realistic surfaces

diagrams.title: Lambert's Cosine Law
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how light intensity varies with angle between surface normal and light direction
elements: Visual representation of N·L dot product
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing why surfaces appear brighter when directly facing light source

diagrams.title: Specular Reflection Vector Geometry
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between incident light, surface normal, reflection direction, and view direction for specular highlights
elements: Angle θ between R and V (key for specular calculation)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how specular highlights appear only when view aligns with reflection direction

diagrams.title: Shading Methods Comparison
diagrams.description: Comparison of Flat, Gouraud, and Phong shading on a sphere showing interpolated normals and highlights
elements: Gouraud: smooth gradient but blurry highlight
Phong: smooth surface with sharp highlight
diagrams.purpose: Choosing appropriate shading method based on quality vs performance requirements
